response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable senior/staff engineer with 15+ years of backend and distributed-systems experience, but the CTO role demands executive leadership capabilities he has not demonstrated at any point in his career. There is no evidence of managing or scaling engineering teams, developing a technical leadership layer, governing outsourced delivery partners, or presenting roadmaps to a board. His agentic-AI exposure is nascent (current Naver role began April 2025) and scoped to developer tooling and MCP code generators rather than production multi-agent systems with real financial consequences. His single fintech engagement (RIDE Capital, 15 months as an IC) shows no credit-risk, payment-flow, real-time exposure-modeling, or compliance depth. The gap between senior individual contributor and venture-backed executive is disqualifying for this role.
strengths: Demonstrated distributed-systems and high-throughput backend depth, including low-latency microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby
Active, hands-on exposure to agentic frameworks and MCP tooling at Naver, showing current relevance to AI-agent infrastructure trends
Consistent polyglot engineering track record (TypeScript, DLang, Node.js, C/C++) with an emphasis on testability, CI/CD, and craftsmanship practices
concerns: Zero engineering leadership experience at any level — no record of hiring, managing teams, developing senior technical leaders, or scaling an organization
Agentic AI work is limited to tooling/framework support (~1 month tenure) with no evidence of designing or operating production multi-agent systems in regulated or high-stakes financial contexts

No meaningful fintech infrastructure depth — the RIDE Capital role was a single IC engagement with no mention of credit systems, dynamic risk modeling, payment flows, or multi-jurisdictional compliance

key_factors.factor: No engineering leadership or team-scaling history
No production multi-agent AI system ownership
